## Key claims

- `clm-0007` (theological) — Dispensational frames can make Middle East conflict feel morally mandatory to some US publics.…
- `clm-0008` (causal) — Christian Zionism can align evangelical energy with Israeli state aims.…
- `clm-0011` (causal) — Saudi–Iran rivalry channels great-power competition without dissolving local agency.…
- `clm-0014` (causal) — Escalation ladders toward Iran can entangle the US via allies and public moral language.…
- `clm-0015` (predictive) — Large-scale Iran invasion scenarios remain thinkable in strategic discourse even when costly.…
- `clm-0016` (descriptive) — Leadership accidents can be read strategically even when facts are opaque.…
- `clm-0017` (anthropological) — Putinism packages sovereignty as cultural survival against Western consumerism tropes.…
- `clm-0018` (descriptive) — Strategic patience can be taught via historical analogy (e.g., nested games).…
- `clm-0019` (predictive) — Domestic polarization can cross thresholds where violence becomes thinkable as 'civil war' metaphor.…
- `clm-0020` (descriptive) — Machine-learning pedagogy can be framed as early psychohistory craft.…
- `clm-0023` (historical) — Christianization paths mix mission networks with imperial co-option stories.…
- `clm-0024` (causal) — Israel can leverage Christian Zionist energy in US coalition politics.…
- `clm-0027` (descriptive) — Gulf monarchies sit between US power projection and regional rivals.…
- `clm-0028` (causal) — Sanctions and deterrence create incentives for asymmetry and narrative combat.…
- `clm-0029` (theological) — Premillennial timelines can accelerate appetite for crisis narratives.…
- `clm-0030` (predictive) — Psychohistory-style AI remains decades away in any strong sense.…
